marapendi
  • Models
  • View page source

Models

Orchestration classes that combine a Cell and a CellState to compute the cell’s behaviour: membrane water balance, gas transport, voltage and thermal sub-models. ExplicitSteadyStateModel composes these into a single explicit steady-state evaluation.

Cell model orchestration.

CellModel operates on a Cell and a CellState to compute the cell’s behaviour. ExplicitSteadyStateModel is the concrete steady-state implementation: it composes the gas-transport, water-balance, voltage and thermal sub-models into a single explicit evaluation.

class marapendi.model.CellModel(water_balance_model: ~marapendi.water_balance.MembraneWaterBalanceModel = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Base class orchestrating calculations on a Cell.

steady_state_solution(conditions)[source]

Compute the steady-state solution of the cell for given operating conditions.

transient_solution(conditions)[source]

Compute the transient solution of the cell for given operating conditions.

water_balance_model: MembraneWaterBalanceModel
class marapendi.model.ExplicitSteadyStateModel(water_balance_model: ~marapendi.water_balance.MembraneWaterBalanceModel = <factory>, cell: ~marapendi.cell.Cell = <factory>, thermal_model: ~marapendi.thermal.ThermalModel = <factory>, gas_transport_model: ~marapendi.transport.GasTransportModel = <factory>, voltage_model: ~marapendi.voltage.VoltageModel = <factory>)[source]

Bases: CellModel

Simplified steady-state model where all calculations are explicit.

cell: Cell
gas_transport_model: GasTransportModel
initial_state(conditions: CellOperatingConditions) → CellState[source]

Initialize a CellState from the operating conditions.

steady_state_solution(conditions) → CellState[source]

Compute the steady-state solution of the cell for given operating conditions.

thermal_model: ThermalModel
voltage_model: VoltageModel

Membrane water balance model.

MembraneWaterBalanceModel computes the steady-state water content profile across the membrane and the resulting water fluxes at the catalyst-layer/membrane interfaces, given a Cell and its CellState.

All physical variables are read from state (or computed from cell.membrane correlations); only the steady-state solution path is implemented.

Notes

The class is based on the equations and assumptions in Ferrara et al. (2018), extended to account for gas transport resistance and non-equilibrium conditions at the membrane interfaces.

References

Ferrara, A. et al. J. Power Sources 390, 197-207 (2018).

class marapendi.water_balance.MembraneWaterBalanceModel(sorption_activity_driving_force: bool = False, eod_parallel_to_sorption: bool = False)[source]

Bases: object

Steady-state membrane water balance.

sorption_activity_driving_force

Whether water activity (rather than water content difference) is the driving force for water absorption (default is False).

Type:

bool, optional

eod_parallel_to_sorption

Whether electro-osmotic drag adds to the water absorption flux on the right-hand side of the water balance boundary conditions (default is False).

Type:

bool, optional

calculate_anode_membrane_flux(state: CellState) → float[source]

Water flux at the anode/membrane interface (kmol/m^2/s).

calculate_biot_number(absorption_coefficient: float, membrane, water_diffusivity: float) → float[source]

Biot number from the absorption coefficient, membrane thickness and water diffusivity.

calculate_cathode_membrane_flux(state: CellState) → float[source]

Water flux at the cathode/membrane interface (kmol/m^2/s).

calculate_peclet_number(membrane, temperature: float, current_density: float, water_diffusivity: float) → float[source]

Peclet number from the electroosmotic drag speed, membrane thickness and water diffusivity.

calculate_water_saturation(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → None[source]

Update the liquid water saturation of the cathode porous layers.

Cathode-only, following Ferrara et al. (2018).

The catalyst layer’s non-wetting saturation has already been set by _solve_liquid_equilibrated_balance(). The GDL’s (and MPL’s, if present) non-wetting saturation is derived here from the cathode liquid water flux via PorousLayer.non_wetting_saturation_from_flux(), chaining the downstream capillary pressure from GDL to MPL. The liquid saturation of every cathode porous layer is then set from its non-wetting saturation, according to its wettability.

calculate_water_transport(cell: Cell, state: CellState, gas_transport_model: GasTransportModel) → None[source]

Update the water vapor transport resistances and solve the membrane water balance.

For each side, computes the H2O gas transport resistance, solves the membrane water balance, and sets the catalyst-layer ionomer water content to its equilibrium value. The resulting cathode liquid water flux is then used to update the liquid water saturation of the cathode porous layers, and the H2O gas transport resistances are recomputed to reflect it.

eod_parallel_to_sorption: bool = False
estimate_equilibrium_water_contents(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → None[source]

Estimate the catalyst-layer equilibrium water content and its derivative, assuming no water crossover from cathode to anode.

solve_water_balance(cell: Cell, state: CellState)[source]

Solve the steady-state membrane water balance and update state in place.

Returns:

The membrane water content profile (also stored in state.membrane.water_content_profile).

Return type:

np.ndarray

sorption_activity_driving_force: bool = False
update_cell_side_water_fluxes(side_state: CellSideState) → None[source]

Split the total water flux of side_state into liquid and vapor fluxes, limited by the maximum vapor removal rate of the flow channel.

update_non_dimensional_parameters(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → None[source]

Compute the Peclet number, Biot number and other non-dimensional parameters used by update_water_profile().

update_water_contents(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → None[source]

Update the membrane average water content and the catalyst-layer equilibrium water contents from the current water content profile.

update_water_fluxes(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → None[source]

Compute the membrane, total, liquid and vapor water fluxes for both sides.

update_water_profile(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → None[source]

Compute the membrane water content profile and its derivative from the cathode/anode equilibrium water contents and non-dimensional parameters.

Gas transport model: gas-phase species transport resistance of a cell side.

class marapendi.transport.GasTransportModel[source]

Bases: object

Computes the gas transport resistance of a CellSide.

calculate_gas_concentrations(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → None[source]

Update the catalyst-layer gas composition for both cell sides.

Reactant (O2/H2) and water vapor concentrations at the catalyst layer are derived from the channel concentrations, the reactant consumption (from state.current_density) and the water vapor flux from the membrane water balance. Requires side_state.h2ov_transport_resistance and side_state.vapor_flux to already be set (i.e. after calculate_water_transport()).

gas_transport_resistance(side: CellSide, side_state: CellSideState, species: str = 'o2') → float[source]

Total gas transport resistance for species (s/m), from the channel to the catalyst layer.

Includes the porous layers, the flow channel and, for O2, the catalyst-layer ionomer film resistance.

Voltage model: reversible voltage, overpotentials and resulting cell voltage.

VoltageModel computes the cell voltage of a Cell for a given CellState.

class marapendi.voltage.VoltageModel[source]

Bases: object

Stateless collection of voltage-related calculations.

activation_overpotential(cell: Cell, state: CellState, theta_PtO: float = 0) → float[source]
calculate_h2_permeation_flux(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → float[source]
calculate_theta_PtO(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → float[source]
compute_cell_voltage(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → CellState[source]

Compute the cell voltage for state, writing results back into it.

high_frequency_resistance(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → float[source]
ohmic_overpotential(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → float[source]
reversible_cell_voltage(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → float[source]
reversible_voltage_vs_RHE(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → float[source]

Thermal model: heat transfer resistance and MEA temperature.

class marapendi.thermal.ThermalModel[source]

Bases: object

Computes the heat transfer resistance and MEA temperature of a Cell.

heat_transfer_resistance(cell: Cell) → float[source]

Overall heat transfer resistance of the MEA (m^2 K / W).

The cathode and anode sides act as parallel thermal resistances.

mea_temperature(cell: Cell, state: CellState) → float[source]

Estimate of the MEA temperature, assuming a fixed fraction of the cell voltage loss is converted to heat (eq. explicit_steady_state_model, mea_temperature_estimation=False).

set_mea_temperature(mea_temperature: float, state: CellState) → None[source]

Set the MEA temperature on the membrane and catalyst layers of state.

The membrane and the catalyst layers are assumed to be at the same temperature.

side_heat_transfer_resistance(side: CellSide) → float[source]

Heat transfer resistance between the MEA and the channel, on one side (m^2 K / W).
